original: FLAT: Flat-LAttice Transformer for Chinese named entity recognition (NER) outperforms other lexicon-based models in performance and efficiency.

top_k.sentence: Levenshtein Transformer achieves comparable performance but much-improved efficiency on both generation and refinement tasks.

FLAT: Flat-LAttice Transformer for Chinese named entity recognition (NER) outperforms other lexicon-based models in performance and efficiency.

The intra-layer model parallel approach enables training transformer models with billions of parameters efficiently, achieving state-of-the-art results on various datasets.
The Reformer model performs on par with Transformer models while being much more memory-efficient and much faster on long sequences.
Star-Transformer achieves significant improvements against the standard Transformer for modestly sized datasets.

Neutron implementation of the Transformer translation model provides comparable performance with interesting features while maintaining readability.

The enhanced transformer model will significantly improve content correctness and performance in data-to-text generation tasks compared to current state-of-the-art systems.

The adaptive universal transformer with recurrence and adaptive computation time achieved a test accuracy of 84.9% in evaluating mathematical expressions.
Modeling recurrence for Transformer with an additional recurrence encoder will improve the translation capacity compared to the standard Transformer model.

TENER, an adapted Transformer Encoder, is just as effective for named entity recognition as other natural language processing tasks.
Retrieving target sequential information for Non-Autoregressive Transformer will enhance its translation ability while preserving the fast-decoding property.

Neural Architecture Transformer (NAT) will significantly outperform both its original form and architectures optimized by existing methods in terms of performance.
The proposed 2D convolutional neural network model yields excellent results, outperforming state-of-the-art encoder-decoder systems, while being conceptually simpler and having fewer parameters.
The proposed encoder demonstrates very promising results both as a standalone encoder and as a complementary building block for reading comprehension tasks.
Scheduled sampling technique will improve model performance in Transformer architectures by applying structural changes via a two-pass decoding strategy.

The attention mechanism in Transformer can be formulated as applying kernel smoother over the inputs, leading to a better understanding of its components and achieving competitive performance with less computation.

Speech-Transformer will achieve competitive word error rate (WER) of 10.9% while being significantly faster than recurrent sequence-to-sequence models.
The set of pre-training tasks significantly improves the performance of embedding-based Transformer models over widely-used BM-25 and embedding models without Transformers.

original: Incorporating multimodal cues can improve the automatic classification of sarcasm.

Leveraging natural language relations between answer choices will improve the performance of machine comprehension.
Using n-tuple assertions in a knowledge-based question-answering system will effectively answer complex questions with rich semantic constraints.
The novel algorithm for textbook question answering significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods.
Automatically extracting evidence from heterogeneous knowledge sources will improve commonsense question answering performance.
The verbalizer-retriever-reader framework for open-domain question answering can effectively benefit from the expanded knowledge index, leading to large gains over text-only baselines.
Human-in-the-loop Question Decomposition can potentially provide an alternate path to building large Language Models.
Generating questions for question answering training by applying a simple template on a related, retrieved sentence improves downstream question answering performance.
Fuzzy relation matching based on statistical models will significantly outperform state-of-the-art density-based passage retrieval methods in question answering systems.
Retrieval Question-Answering (ReQA) will serve as a benchmark for evaluating large-scale sentence-level answer retrieval models.
Augmenting a text-to-text model with a large memory of question-answer pairs will improve performance on smaller question-answering benchmarks.
The novel approach to answering sequential questions based on structured objects will yield competitive results on the Sequential Question Answering task.
Deep Thinking Question Answering (DTQA) achieves state-of-the-art performance on two popular Knowledge Base Question Answering datasets.
Evaluating semantic parsing-based question answering models will yield reasonable performance compared to a web-based question answering baseline.
Recent methods for complex knowledge base question answering will address the challenges of answering complex questions more effectively than earlier approaches.
Enhancing key-value memory neural networks will improve reasoning abilities on complex questions in knowledge based question answering.
Incorporating easy, yet diverse samples can further improve learning in question answering models.
Improving question answering systems post-deployment based on user interactions enhances both accuracy and explainability.
The graph-based recurrent retrieval approach will achieve state-of-the-art results in multi-hop open-domain question answering.
HyperLink-induced Pre-training (HLP) will significantly improve passage retrieval accuracy compared to existing pre-training methods in open-domain question answering.
The proposed semantic parsing framework for question answering using a knowledge base will outperform previous methods substantially.
Knowledge-Enriched Answer Generator (KEAG) improves answer quality over models without knowledge and existing knowledge-aware models.
A question-answering system can learn to relate concepts in science questions to propositions in a fact corpus and generalize to other domains.
The joint passage retrieval model (JPR) achieves significantly better answer coverage on three multi-answer datasets compared to prior approaches.
The proposed algorithm with a novel reader-retriever design will achieve state-of-the-art accuracy in open-domain question answering.
Incorporating additional commonsense knowledge into language model based approaches will improve question answering performance in commonsense question answering tasks.
Querying language models by example significantly improves their performance on factual and commonsense knowledge retrieval.

given_idea: By leveraging the MIMIC-RRS dataset, researchers can evaluate the effectiveness of radiology report summarization models in a more comprehensive manner, enabling them to identify areas where individual model components, such as factual correctness, require refinement.
original: Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.
The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.

top_k.sentence: The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.
A unified framework for exploiting both extra knowledge and the original findings in an integrated way will facilitate impression generation in radiology reports.
Incorporating content planning in a summarization model yields output of higher quality and allows the creation of synthetic datasets resembling real-world document-summary pairs.
New approaches that capitalize on domain-specific models will modestly improve the factual accuracy of generated summaries.
Data-derived hypotheses may help to explain what types of reframes are helpful for users.
MIMIC-CXR is a large dataset of chest radiographs that facilitates research in computer vision, natural language processing, and clinical data mining.
The RadGraph reward method will improve the factual completeness and correctness of generated radiology reports compared to existing methods.
Pretrained models are better summarizers in generating faithful and factual summaries as evaluated by humans.

Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.

Introducing new rewards to encourage the generation of factually complete and consistent radiology reports will significantly improve the performance of image-to-text radiology report generation systems.

The proposed two-step approach for radiology report summarization leads to a more precise summary compared to single-step and two-step-with-single-extractive-process baselines with an overall improvement in F1 score of 3-4%.
The proposed method is effective and surpasses state-of-the-art systems on the standard summarization dataset.
Cross-dataset evaluation will provide a better understanding of the generalization ability of different summarization systems compared to in-domain evaluation.
Automating the generation of radiology impressions with neural sequence-to-sequence learning will significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of summarizing radiology findings.
Reinforced cross-modal alignment for radiology report generation will improve the quality of generated reports by better aligning visual and textual features.
The template-based multi-attention report generation model (TMRGM) is able to simulate the reporting process for chest X-ray image interpretation.

The radiology report generation model utilizing the transformer architecture produces superior reports as measured by both standard language generation and clinical coherence metrics compared to competitive baselines.
Summarization Programs improve the interpretability of summarization models by allowing humans to better simulate model reasoning.
An end-to-end evaluation framework for expansion-based interactive summarization will improve the customization and comparison of summary results.
The integration of information extraction and radiology information systems has been demonstrated to be effective in improving the efficiency of reading radiology reports.
The new dataset for joint Text Simplification and Summarization is well suited to simplification.
A deep understanding of the characteristics of datasets can bring significant improvements to the existing state-of-the-art model in neural extractive summarization.
FactGraph will improve factual consistency in summarization compared to previous approaches.
The domain-aware automatic chest X-ray radiology report generation system will improve clinical accuracy and readability compared to existing methods.
Enhancing factual robustness will improve the faithfulness of Seq2Seq summarization models.
An adversarial meta-evaluation methodology will diagnose the strengths and weaknesses of existing factuality metrics for summarization.
The dataset and initial analysis are designed to support future work by fake news researchers.
Extensions to state-of-the-art summarization approaches will achieve substantially better results on the EntSUM data set for controllable summarization.
Annotations marking the evidence in existing TyDi QA and BoolQ datasets can be used to train a model that extracts improved evidence spans compared to models that rely on existing resources.
Targeted summarization will improve the accuracy of summarization tasks compared to traditional methods that summarize entire documents.
A Word Graph guided Summarization model (WGSum) will generate high-quality impressions for radiology findings by accurately capturing important words and their relations.

A data-driven approach for performing automated quality assessment of radiologic interpretations will achieve high precision and recall in matching radiologic interpretations with pathology diagnoses.
The post-editing corrector module will correct factual errors in summaries generated by neural summarization models and outperform previous models on factual consistency evaluation.
A new approach to human evaluation will improve the consistency and transparency of multi-document summarisation in the biomedical literature.
Utilizing a pre-constructed graph embedding module on multiple disease findings will assist the generation of radiology reports and improve reporting quality.
Generating hard, representative synthetic examples of non-factual summaries through infilling language models will improve factual consistency in abstractive summarization.
A model-based metric will estimate the factual accuracy of generated text that is complementary to typical scoring schemes like ROUGE and BLEU.
The dataset introduced will aid medical text simplification research by providing aligned complex medical sentences and their simplified versions.
NLI models can be effective for recognizing factual inconsistency in summarization when the training data is augmented with high-quality task-oriented examples.
The proposed model is beneficial for multi-modal summarization and superior to existing methods.
The Multi-Document Summarization of Medical Studies (MS²) dataset facilitates the development of systems that can assess and aggregate contradictory evidence across multiple studies.
A weakly-supervised, model-based approach for verifying factual consistency will substantially outperform previous models in assessing the factual consistency of abstractive text summarization.
Reporting additional details, especially performance on validation data obtained during model development, will lead to more accurate conclusions about model performance.
Readability models can be useful for identifying simplification targets for human writers and for evaluating machine generated simplifications.
The method will outperform similarity-based methods on datasets in a range of domains including news, medical journal articles, and personal anecdotes.
The Posterior-and-Prior Knowledge Exploring-and-Distilling approach (PPKED) will outperform previous state-of-the-art models in generating radiology reports on MIMIC-CXR and IU-Xray datasets.
Scientific papers are suitable for data-driven text summarization.
Entity-based SpanCopy mechanism will improve factual consistency in abstractive summarization by reducing mismatched entities between generated summaries and source documents.
The introduction of the NEWTS corpus will enable more effective evaluation of topic-focused summarization models compared to existing datasets.
End-to-end multimodal fact-checking and explanation generation will improve the assessment of claim truthfulness compared to existing methods.

given_idea: When sustainability initiatives prioritize individual behavioral change without addressing the underlying social and cultural factors that drive consumption patterns, they may inadvertently create a sense of disconnection and responsibility diffusion, ultimately undermining the effectiveness of their efforts.
original: ConSERT achieves an 8% relative improvement over the previous state-of-the-art on semantic textual similarity tasks.
The proposed method using contextualised word representations can effectively detect and quantify lexical semantic change in diachronic text collections.

top_k.sentence: Social norms evolve in ways that depend upon the psychological dispositions of the individual and may impair social efficiency.
Values motivate behavior, but the relation between values and behaviors is partly obscured by norms.

An external intervention should stay close to the potential exiter's own doubt, make the influence attempt as subtle as possible, use narratives and self-affirmatory strategies to reduce resistance to persuasion, and consider the possibility to promote attitudinal change via behavioral change as an alternative to seek to influence beliefs directly.

Providing explanations could potentially mitigate misplaced trust in the system and over-reliance.
Reclamation programmes can inhibit participants who do not identify with the cultural values or practices they promote.

The qualitative ordering of sacrifice acceptability is best explained by basic cognitive processes rather than cultural norms, while low relational mobility is strongly associated with the rejection of sacrifices for the greater good, particularly in Eastern countries.
Social-contextual factors nurture intrinsic motivation and promote internalization, leading to desired educational outcomes.
The degree of contextual dependence of interpersonal verbs significantly affects the strength of causality implicit in such verbs.
The dimensions of coordination, purviews, and constraints are aspects of grounding that are missing from NLP tasks.
Representations encoded in models often inadvertently perpetuate undesirable social biases from the data on which they are trained.

Homophily limits people's social worlds in a way that has powerful implications for the information they receive, the attitudes they form, and the interactions they experience.

Behavioral activation (BA) has been investigated over time and its emerging application to a broad range of populations and problems.
The discourse on the social implications of natural language processing has not kept pace with technological development, necessitating a discussion on its ethical significance.
The framing of decision problems significantly influences preference shifts in evaluations of probabilities and outcomes.
The tailoring-ingredients influence persuasion through different psychological processes.
Misconceptions regarding de-identification are perpetuated, undermining privacy and privacy norms.
AI systems are not guaranteed to take decisions that are more objective than those of a human, but those systems can still be used to reduce social inequalities.

Social biases are present in grounded embeddings and seem to be equally or more significant than for ungrounded embeddings.

'This contribution attempts to sensitize researchers in the social and behavioral sciences to the importance of definitional issues and their consequences for distinguishing related but fundamentally different affective processes, states, and traits.'

given_idea: By leveraging similar examples with n-gram overlap, translators can consistently produce high-quality outputs that meet the demands of the target domain.
original: The translation quality and the domain of the in-context examples matter, and adding similar examples based on an n-gram overlap with the test source significantly and consistently improves the translation quality of the outputs.

top_k.sentence: The translation quality and the domain of the in-context examples matter, and adding similar examples based on an n-gram overlap with the test source significantly and consistently improves the translation quality of the outputs.
Pre-training n-gram-enhanced encoders with a large volume of data and advanced techniques will improve performance on various downstream tasks across languages and domains.
Using N-gram models as a postprocessing step will enhance the generation performance in machine translation.
The amount of reordering, the morphological complexity of the target language, and the historical relatedness of the two languages are strong predictors of performance in machine translation systems.
The Montreal neural machine translation systems will achieve results comparable to the state of the art on a greater variety of language pairs.
Training high quality word representations for 157 languages on large corpora will lead to strong performance in natural language processing tasks.
The method will outperform competitive baselines on multiple data sets in domain adaptation for neural machine translation.

The parallel corpus of scientific publications for the biomedical domain will achieve performance that rivals or exceeds the state of the art in the biomedical domain when used in machine translation.
Web n-gram features will give significant gains on multiple datasets and metrics for the task of coreference resolution.
The proposed techniques produce consistent and large improvements in translation quality, over baseline models based on prior work.
The presented data harvesting method produces a parallel corpus of high quality, and the supervised system trained on this corpus significantly outperforms a strong baseline.
Both characters and embeddings achieve good performance in scoring the outputs of machine translation systems.
THUMT can obtain results that are comparable to state-of-the-art neural machine translation systems.
Combining extraction and generation for jargon definition modeling will generate high-quality definitions for jargon and outperform state-of-the-art models significantly.
Integrating translations for unseen words into a phrase-based translation system will yield consistent improvements in translation quality.
Partial sharing of parameters can lead to substantial improvements in translation accuracy.
The performance of statistical machine translation systems varies greatly with the source and target language.
Utilizing multiple human references and generating pseudo-references will lead to substantial improvements in machine translation and image captioning performance.
Constructing a large number of derivations that include contextually richer rules and account for multiple interpretations of unaligned words will improve translation model performance.
Pretrained transformers, such as BERT, produce high quality results across many domains, tasks, and settings in text ranking.
Using sentence codes to condition the sentence generation will result in higher diversity scores for sampled translations while maintaining translation quality.
Incorporating word and subword units in unsupervised machine translation will improve the fluency and consistency of translations.
Multi-domain neural machine translation approach will result in significant translation quality gains over fine-tuning.
Exploiting large but cheap monolingual in-domain data will lead to significant performance gains in statistical machine translation.

Training a Transformer model to produce both accurate translations and alignments will yield competitive results compared to GIZA++ trained IBM alignment models without sacrificing translation accuracy.
The inclusion of both syntactic and semantic information can improve translation quality.
The resulting model has state-of-the-art translation performance and uses linguistically motivated features to effectively model long range reordering.
Combining output from identical statistical machine translation systems trained on alternative morphological decompositions of the source language will achieve translation performance improvements.
ASR word lattices can be translated even when exhaustive reordering is required for good translation quality.
Domain control for neural machine translation will show quality improvements compared to dedicated domains translating on any of the covered domains and even on out-of-domain data.
The use of 3-gram language models is highly recommended in statistical machine translation.
The method achieves high precision and recall and outperforms commercial machine translation systems.
Using context in document-level machine translation will lead to improvements in translation quality.
Large, pre-trained language models will achieve state-of-the-art performance for numerous natural language processing tasks.
The dual-path simultaneous machine translation method can outperform strong baselines under all latency.
Bridging the word order gap leads to significant improvement in translation quality in extremely low-resource scenarios.
Building an initial system on a concatenation of available out-of-domain data and then fine-tuning it on in-domain data achieves the best translation quality.
Understanding semantic capacity of terms will help many downstream tasks in natural language processing.
Incorporating pseudo-references with fewer reorderings will improve simultaneous translation performance.
Orthogonal transformations can be as good a representation of word relationships as translation vectors.
The proposed domain alignment learns a semantically meaningful representation which outperforms previous work.
The approach exploits knowledge encoded in lexical resources to induce representations for rare and unseen words, providing consistent improvements on multiple benchmarks across domains.
The architecture for machine translation will achieve substantial improvements over strong bilingual models and perform well for zero-shot translation.
The memory-enhanced neural machine translation method will significantly outperform strong baseline models in translation quality, especially in handling troublesome words.
Neural machine translation techniques can be a powerful tool for modeling sequential data.
Training criteria that directly optimize translation quality will yield significantly better results compared to traditional maximum likelihood approaches.
Multilingual trained models outperform the translation pipeline and are on par with the monolingual models.
Using target-side monolingual data to extend the vocabulary of the translation model improves the decoder's ability to produce grammatically correct translations in small-data settings.
Processing machine translation input with named entity recognition technology will improve the quality of machine translation.
Multitask learning is useful in machine translation quality estimation, leading to improved results in all settings.

given_idea: By employing a two-stage approach that combines bidirectional long short-term memory networks with margin loss, researchers expect to achieve significant advancements in identifying novel user intentions, outperforming traditional methods.
original: The two-stage method using bidirectional long short-term memory network with margin loss will yield consistent improvements in detecting unknown user intents compared to baseline methods.

top_k.sentence: The two-stage method using bidirectional long short-term memory network with margin loss will yield consistent improvements in detecting unknown user intents compared to baseline methods.

Agreement on target-bidirectional long short-term memory networks will generate more balanced targets and achieve consistent and substantial improvements in sequence-to-sequence learning tasks compared to existing systems.

Combining Convolutional Neural Networks with Long Short-Term Memory networks will significantly outperform traditional approaches based on signal processing techniques for the prediction of spontaneous and natural emotions.
The model based on bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks outperforms other participants on detecting errors in learner writing.

The stacked bidirectional Long-Short Term Memory network will outperform previous methods that require syntactic features and external knowledge resources in answer sentence selection for question answering.
A bidirectional long short-term memory network will achieve statistically equivalent results to the best state-of-the-art systems for word sense disambiguation without using external resources.

The deep bidirectional long short-term memory model with a connectionist temporal classification layer achieves a word error rate of 21.3%, which is a 9% relative improvement compared to the previous best word error rate of 23.4% from a hybrid system.

A simple Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory architecture with appropriate regularization yields accuracy and F1 that are either competitive or exceed the state of the art on four standard benchmark datasets.

The combination of Convolutional Neural Networks and Long Short Term Memory networks will outperform each individual model as well as several baselines in identifying empathetic messages in online health communities.
Long short term memory recurrent networks after pretrained with the two approaches become more stable to train and generalize better.
Maximum margin Markov networks will significantly improve performance on tasks involving sequential, spatial, or structured data compared to existing kernel-based methods.
A neural network approach based on Long Short-Term Memory networks will achieve state-of-the-art performance in the quote recommendation task compared to existing methods.
Bidirectional long short-term memory models will perform better for part-of-speech tagging across multiple languages and data sizes compared to traditional part-of-speech taggers.
The modified Long Short-Term Memory network with jumping will be up to 6 times faster than the standard sequential Long Short-Term Memory network while maintaining the same or better accuracy.
Learning parameters in structured prediction models using large margin estimation will produce significant improvements in inference accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods.
Integrating topic information into bidirectional long short-term memory networks outperforms vanilla BiLSTMs by more than 3 percentage points in F1 in two- and three-label cross-topic settings.
Multi-label transfer learning approach based on LSTM will outperform the single-task approach and the traditional multi-task learning approach in predicting multiple relations simultaneously.
Neural sequence models like Long Short-Term Memory networks are able to discover and implicitly use recursive compositional structure for tasks with clear cues to that structure in the data.
The long short-term memory (LSTM) cell can effectively handle long-term dependencies in sequential data compared to traditional recurrent neural networks.
Dynamic memory networks (DMN) will obtain state-of-the-art results on question answering, text classification for sentiment analysis, and sequence modeling for part-of-speech tagging tasks.
A neural network architecture will perform on par with earlier classifiers for negation scope detection, with a bi-directional LSTM outperforming all of them.
Ensemble LSTM will improve user classification by incorporating multiple textual perspectives compared to previous methods that model textual features in a single perspective.
Using Recurrent Neural Networks will result in substantial improvements in prediction performance on knowledge tracing datasets.
The multi-topic aware long short-term memory network (MTA-LSTM) will generate essays that are coherent and closely related to the input topics, outperforming various baselines in terms of BLEU score.

The novel deep architecture will utilize both structural and textual information of entities to outperform baseline models and obtain state-of-the-art results on link prediction and triplet classification tasks.
A fully automated framework using Long-Short Term Memory networks will achieve excellent results in Automated Text Scoring compared to similar approaches.
TapNets, neural networks augmented with task-adaptive projection, will improve few-shot learning performance compared to traditional methods.

Multiplicative tree-structured long short-term memory networks will provide more expressive semantic representations by utilizing both word and relation information compared to existing tree-structured models.
Combining deep learning with active learning can outperform classical methods even with a significantly smaller amount of training data.
Active Deep Networks will outperform existing semi-supervised learning algorithms and deep learning techniques for sentiment classification.
Knowledge transfer method via distillation of activation boundaries formed by hidden neurons will outperform the current state-of-the-art.

Small and shallow feed-forward neural networks can achieve near state-of-the-art results on a range of unstructured and structured language processing tasks while being considerably cheaper in memory and computational requirements than deep recurrent models.
Co-Factorization Machines will outperform state-of-the-art methods in predicting user decisions and modeling user interests in social media.
The neural model that reads two sentences to determine entailment using long short-term memory units will outperform previous models and classifiers in recognizing entailment relations.
The strong performance of convolutional networks and self-attentional networks could also be due to their ability to extract semantic features from the source text.
An attention-based LSTM model will achieve competitive results in fine-grained information status classification compared to state-of-the-art approaches that rely on hand-crafted semantic features.

A predictive, continuous model of turn-taking using Long Short-Term Memory Recurrent Neural Networks will achieve better performance than human observers in predicting turn-shifts and will be applicable to different tasks not specifically trained for.
The novel neural network architecture using hybrid bidirectional LSTM and CNN will achieve state of the art performance in named entity recognition tasks compared to existing approaches.
Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) can effectively and efficiently embed text regions of variable sizes for text categorization in supervised and semi-supervised settings.
Conditionally shifted neurons will enable artificial neural networks to achieve rapid adaptation to new tasks with little data, resulting in state-of-the-art performance on metalearning benchmarks.
Long short-term memory neural networks will outperform simple recurrent neural networks and convolutional neural networks in the word labeling task.
Minimizing the prediction divergence between the two complementary networks can lead to significant performance gains in semi-supervised learning.
Deep learning techniques will yield state-of-the-art performance in named entity recognition compared to traditional methods.
Long Short-Term Memory will improve named entity recognition performance by utilizing two passes for disambiguation.
Long Short-Term Memory networks will exhibit varying inductive learning capabilities for simple formal languages based on training data regimes and model capacity.

Temporal convolution combined with unidirectional long short-term memory layers will limit latency to 200 ms while outperforming state-of-the-art low frame rate bidirectional long short-term memory models.
Bidirectional long short-term memory (LSTM) will automatically learn orthographic features for named entity recognition in Twitter messages without requiring feature engineering.
Bidirectional LSTM models achieve highly competitive performance to the state-of-the-art results on caption generation and significantly outperform recent methods on retrieval task.

A bidirectional LSTM with an attention mechanism will improve clickbait detection by learning the contribution of words to a post's clickbait score, and incorporating image embeddings will enhance the model's performance.
Long short term memory networks equipped with an attention mechanism will effectively predict prominent counselor and client behaviors in addiction counseling sessions.

given_idea: The FewRel dataset has been a crucial testing ground for the MLMAN, a network that has successfully adapted to new, unseen data and achieved state-of-the-art results in few-shot relation classification.
original: Multi-level matching and aggregation network (MLMAN) achieves a new state-of-the-art performance on the FewRel dataset for few-shot relation classification.

top_k.sentence: Multi-level matching and aggregation network (MLMAN) achieves a new state-of-the-art performance on the FewRel dataset for few-shot relation classification.
Prototypical Networks will achieve excellent results in few-shot classification compared to recent approaches.
The Relation Network (RN) provides a unified and effective approach for few-shot learning and can also be extended to zero-shot learning.
The combination of better supervised data and a more appropriate high-capacity model enables much better relation extraction performance.

The method for learning embeddings for few-shot learning achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard few-shot benchmark datasets.

The scale and the real-world nature of the MS MARCO dataset makes it attractive for benchmarking machine reading comprehension and question-answering models.
The graph neural network architecture generalizes several of the recently proposed few-shot learning models and provides improved numerical performance.

The proposed framework for optimizing the latent representations with respect to the analogical properties of the embedded entities and relations significantly outperformed a large number of representative baseline methods on benchmark datasets.
Deep learning models have the potential to improve relation extraction through the use of large datasets created by distant supervision.
Capsule networks achieve state of the art on 4 out of 6 datasets for text classification.
The convolutional deep neural network will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art methods for relation classification.
The improved method of recovering relational similarities will enhance the state-of-the-art results on word-analogy datasets.
Meta-Dataset will serve as a new benchmark for training and evaluating models in few-shot classification, addressing the limitations of existing procedures and datasets.
Using convolutional neural networks for relation classification will outperform state-of-the-art systems that rely on handcrafted features.
A modified baseline method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art few-shot learning algorithms on both the miniImageNet and the CUB datasets.
A framework that unifies the learning of Relation Extraction and Knowledge Base Embeddings models leads to significant improvements over the state-of-the-art in Relation Extraction.
The novel approach for multi-instance learning with overlapping relations will yield significant gains in accuracy for information extraction tasks.
The proposed framework for extracting shared structures in multi-label classification will demonstrate effectiveness in comparison with several representative algorithms.
A single, joint graphical model will consistently improve results on entity tagging, relation extraction, and coreference compared to isolated models.

The Markov Logic model jointly identifies temporal relations of all three relation types simultaneously, leading to about 2% higher accuracy for all three types of relations compared to other machine learning based systems.
The classifier models learned show statistically significant improvement over the baseline vector space model system on all the collections tested.
An unsupervised method for relation discovery from large corpora will detect relations among named entities with high recall and precision.
Novel extensions of Prototypical Networks will improve predictions in few-shot classification by leveraging unlabeled examples.
The latent variable extractive model outperforms a strong extractive baseline trained on rule-based labels and performs competitively with several recent models.
Few-shot relation classification remains an open problem and still requires further research.
The joint embedding model will achieve significant improvement on relation extraction compared to cutting-edge methods.
The learned classifiers outperform previous approaches for learning with limited data and are comparable with fully supervised classifiers trained from a small number of labeled examples.
MoleculeNet benchmarks demonstrate that learnable representations are powerful tools for molecular machine learning and broadly offer the best performance.

The feature representation learned by the discriminative unsupervised feature learning algorithm achieves classification results matching or outperforming the current state-of-the-art for unsupervised learning on several popular datasets.
Machine learning models trained on features derived from WordNet and the Google N-gram corpus will outperform existing baselines in identifying temporal and causal relations.
The algorithmic framework for learning multiple related tasks will show consistent improvements over baseline methods in shallow parsing and named-entity recognition.
MARINE will effectively learn vector transformations for entities in both homogeneous and multi-relational networks while preserving proximity and relation information.

The maximum entropy framework is able to make use of global information directly, and achieves performance that is comparable to the best previous machine learning-based named entity recognizers on MUC-6 and MUC-7 test data.
CrossE achieves state-of-the-art results on complex and more challenging datasets for link prediction tasks.
The proposed zero-shot learning model can achieve state-of-the-art results on both zero-shot learning and few-shot learning problems.
The curated dataset provides an excellent signal for learning vector representations of sentence meaning.
Latent embedding optimization (LEO) can achieve state-of-the-art performance on few-shot classification tasks.
The proposed method for modeling large multi-relational datasets will attain or outperform state-of-the-art results.
The Induction Network will significantly outperform existing state-of-the-art models in few-shot text classification by improving average accuracy through class-wise generalization.
The unified framework takes advantage of both generative and discriminative models, demonstrating significant performance gains on Precision@5 and MAP in various applications.
The label propagation algorithm achieves better performance than support vector machines when only very few labeled examples are available for relation extraction.
ARNOR framework achieves significant improvements in relation classification performance and noise reduction effect compared to state-of-the-art methods.
The attention-based convolutional neural network architecture will achieve better performance in relation classification compared to state-of-the-art neural network models.
Multi-Grained Named Entity Recognition (MGNER) outperforms current state-of-the-art baselines up to 4.4% in terms of the F1 score among nested/non-overlapping NER tasks.
The results reflect how neural networks discover structure in natural datasets.
The proposed technique for synthesizing samples with multiple labels will facilitate multi-label few-shot classifier learning by enabling augmentation on examples of novel categories.

The new dataset is useful for training and testing supervised machine learning algorithms for multi-label emotion classification, emotion intensity regression, detecting valence, detecting ordinal class of intensity of emotion, and detecting ordinal class of valence.

Neighborhood information significantly helps to improve the results of the TransE model, leading to better performance than obtained by other state-of-the-art embedding models on three benchmark datasets for triple classification, entity prediction and relation prediction tasks.

given_idea: The model's impressive performance in response quality and persona consistency is a testament to its ability to adapt to limited data scenarios.
original: The BERT-over-BERT model outperforms strong baselines in response quality and persona consistency under different limited data settings.

top_k.sentence: Response generation with both paired and unpaired data will significantly outperform several state-of-the-art response generation models when only a few pairs are available.
The proposed model achieves impressive empirical performance on a wide range of entity-related tasks.
Methods that flexibly model the response surface perform better overall than methods that fail to do so.
The controlled response generation mechanism will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art response generation models under both automatic and human evaluations.
The proposed model significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art models in multi-turn response selection tasks.
Humans are able to correctly classify responses at a rate much better than chance, thus confirming that the task is feasible.

Equipping response generation defined by a pre-trained language model with a knowledge selection module will significantly outperform state-of-the-art methods in both automatic evaluation and human judgment.
The model generates informative responses with controlled sentence function and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
The model can generate more appropriate and informative responses than state-of-the-art baselines.
Introducing a KL loss and a style classifier to the fine-tuning step will significantly improve response generation in terms of style consistency and contextual coherence.

The variational neural-based generation model will outperform previous models when having sufficient training dataset and demonstrate strong ability to work acceptably well when the training data is scarce.
Marginalising multiple predictors allows the model to outperform strong benchmarks.
The developed model that combines retrieval and refinement approaches produces responses superior to both standard retrieval and generation models in human evaluations.
Models based on the small high-reliability dataset performed better than models based on the large low-reliability dataset.
PeTra is highly effective in both evaluations, demonstrating its ability to track people in its memory despite being trained with limited annotation.
The BERT-over-BERT model outperforms strong baselines in response quality and persona consistency under different limited data settings.
The existing models perform reasonably well on certain types of questions, but there is a large performance gap compared to human performance.
Combining the written and spoken responses provides a large boost in prediction accuracy.
The Meta-Profile Network shows significant improvement in the model performance when compared to baseline models.
Confident Adaptive Transformers will simultaneously increase computational efficiency while guaranteeing a specifiable degree of consistency with the original model.
Selecting a highly informative subset of data can produce recognition performance comparable to a system that uses a much larger amount of data.
The CorrRNN model will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art method on benchmark datasets in terms of both accuracy and diversity.
An algorithm that customizes a unique dialogue model for each task in the few-shot setting will outperform existing methods in terms of task consistency, response quality, and diversity.
The narrow facets of personality are able to substantially increase the maximum prediction achieved by the broad factors.
A shift in focus from quantity to quality of data could lead to robust models and improved out-of-distribution generalization.
The proposed model generates significantly more diverse responses than baseline approaches and exhibits competence of discourse-level decision-making.

The hredGAN shows improved performance over existing methods by generating longer, more informative, and more diverse responses with high utterance and topic relevance even with limited training data.

High human disagreement exists in a noticeable amount of examples in these datasets, and models achieve near-perfect accuracy on the subset of data with a high level of human agreement, whereas they can barely beat a random guess on the data with low levels of human agreement.

The proposed model shows impressive results on two datasets, i.e. microblogs and news headlines datasets, and performs better than the existing state-of-the-art systems for the above two datasets by 2.0 and 4.1 cosine points, respectively.
The DialKG Walker model can retrieve more natural and human-like responses than the state-of-the-art baselines or rule-based models.
RobBERT improves state-of-the-art results for various tasks, especially significantly outperforms other models when dealing with smaller datasets.
A single model trained concurrently on multiple tasks will yield good results across various domains.
High performance can be achieved with small training datasets, so long as models have appropriate inductive bias or make use of additional unlabeled data or synthetic data.
Pretrained language models achieve state-of-the-art performance when adapted to downstream NLP tasks, and under certain conditions, simple classification heads can solve the downstream task.

Equipping dialog models with background stories related to a persona will generate responses that are more diverse and rated more engaging and human-like compared to outputs from existing dialog models.
PrivBayes will significantly outperform existing solutions in terms of accuracy for releasing high-dimensional data.
Models performing well on the TyDi QA dataset are expected to generalize across a large number of the world’s languages.
The semi-supervised method outperforms a strong supervised baseline when only a small amount of labeled data is available.
Models trained on the novel feature set showed significant increases in accuracy relative to two high baselines.
The proposed model matches the answer with the passage before generating the question and outperforms the existing state of the art using rich features.
Using a small sampled subset (5%-20%) can obtain roughly equal performance compared to the model trained on the entire dataset.
The extractive approach will significantly outperform state of the art on two datasets and have comparable performance on a third.
RankQA achieves state-of-the-art performance on 3 out of 4 benchmark datasets and provides an effective remedy against the underlying noise-information trade-off due to a variable corpus size.
The scale and the real-world nature of the MS MARCO dataset makes it attractive for benchmarking machine reading comprehension and question-answering models.
The pre-training based personalized dialogue model will generate more coherent and persona consistent responses using persona-sparse dialogue data compared to state-of-the-art methods.
The item-response model will identify trustworthy annotators and predict correct underlying labels, matching the performance of more complex state-of-the-art systems.
The models learn to recognize many dialect features with high accuracy, and a few minimal pairs can be as effective for training as thousands of labeled examples.
The neural conversation model generates consistent responses by maintaining certain features related to topics and personas throughout the conversation.
A model trained using semi-supervised self-training can achieve results comparable to a model trained using a much larger set of fully labeled data.
The approach based on exploiting the redundancy of Web information will achieve a high level of performance in answer validation.

given_idea: Zero-shot text style transfer can be successfully executed using large language models in conjunction with augmented zero-shot learning, eliminating the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.
original: Augmented zero-shot learning will effectively perform zero-shot text style transfer using large language models without the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.

top_k.sentence: Augmented zero-shot learning will effectively perform zero-shot text style transfer using large language models without the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.
Learning directly from raw text about images enables zero-shot transfer of the model to downstream tasks.
The probabilistic formulation of unsupervised text style transfer will yield substantial gains over state-of-the-art non-generative baselines across various style transfer tasks.
Zero-shot model adaptation to new tasks using natural language descriptions will achieve significant improvements in classification accuracy on benchmark text classification datasets.
The semi-supervised text style transfer model will effectively combine small-scale parallel data with large-scale nonparallel data to improve the performance of text style transfer.

The proposed framework will significantly outperform five state-of-the-art methods for text style transfer by utilizing a variational auto-encoder and attribute predictors without relying on disentanglement of content and attributes or adversarial learning.
The proposed method leverages refined alignment of latent representations to perform style transfer effectively.

Adapting unsupervised machine translation methods for automatic style transfer will outperform previous state-of-the-art models in terms of accuracy of style transfer and quality of input-output correspondence.
A reinforcement learning based framework will yield better transfer of target style in text style transfer compared to existing state-of-the-art frameworks.
The proposed method utilizes gradients to revise sentences in a continuous space during inference to achieve text style transfer without relying on adversarial learning.
Neural multi-task training for text style transfer will replace common but limited rule-based style transfer systems by more flexible machine learning models.
TextSETTR will enable effective text style transfer using unlabeled data and a style vector space, achieving competitive performance on sentiment transfer compared to models trained on labeled data.
The proposed neural network architecture will fairly transfer multiple style attributes in a given text, outperforming existing one-style transfer methods.
Using a reinforcement-learning-based generator-evaluator architecture will improve text style transfer performance without a parallel training corpus.
Pretrained language models can generate high-quality sentence embeddings without the need for labeled data, finetuning, or modifications to the pretraining objective.
Training algorithms that optimize reward functions considering different aspects of style-transferred outputs will improve text style transfer performance.
Combining zero-shot and translation methods achieves state-of-the-art performance in two of the three datasets used in this work.

Domain adaptive text style transfer models will effectively learn from the source domain to distinguish stylized information and generic content information, maximally preserve content information, and adaptively transfer the styles in a domain-aware manner.
Two strategies can be applied to a multilingual neural machine translation system to better tackle zero-shot scenarios despite not having any parallel corpus.
The fully entangled model produces better generations compared to existing models for unsupervised style transfer in text.
The proposed style transfer scheme achieves distribution-matching style transfer by training only on a self-reconstruction loss.
Meta-tuning directly optimizes the zero-shot learning objective by fine-tuning pre-trained language models on a collection of datasets.
The fully entangled model produces better generations in multiple-attribute text style transfer compared to existing methods.

The finetuning algorithm enables the multi-way, multilingual model to translate a zero-resource language pair as well as a single-pair neural translation model trained with up to 1M direct parallel sentences of the same language pair and better than pivot-based translation strategy.

A systematic review of text style transfer methodologies using deep learning will highlight commonalities and differences between proposed solutions as well as challenges and opportunities in the field.
Training neural language models from scratch will significantly improve zero-shot text-based image retrieval and classification performance compared to attribute-based methods.
The hierarchical reinforced sequence operation method, named Point-Then-Operate, significantly outperforms recent methods in addressing the challenges of unsupervised text style transfer.
Unsupervised text style transfer will effectively translate offensive sentences into non-offensive ones.
The proposed methods outperform current state-of-the-art multilingual multimodal models in zero-shot cross-lingual settings.
Fine-tuning pre-trained language and sequence-to-sequence models boosts content preservation in formality style transfer, even with limited amounts of parallel data.
Cross-lingual pre-training approach will significantly improve zero-shot translation performance compared to existing transfer methods.
An iterative training procedure leveraging duality of translations will improve zero-shot translation performance in low-resource multilingual settings.
Zero-shot Learning approach for text categorization will generalize to unseen sentences, tags, and new datasets.
The proposed approach for zero-shot learning of object categories will successfully predict classifier parameters for new classes using purely textual descriptions.
Style Transformer achieves better style transfer and better content preservation compared to existing neural models in unpaired text style transfer.
The zero-shot modeling framework transfers simplification knowledge from English to another language while generalizing across languages and tasks.
Reformulating unsupervised style transfer as a paraphrase generation problem will significantly outperform state-of-the-art style transfer systems on both human and automatic evaluations.
The proposed methods enable quick and effective adaptation of pretrained multilingual models to low-resource languages and unseen scripts.
The zero-shot learning approach for text classification will effectively predict new domains not observed during training, requiring less storage and performing better than generative baselines.
Removing residual connections in an encoder layer will improve zero-shot translation by leading to more language-independent representations.
Existing methods struggle to model fine-grained changes and have difficulty composing multiple styles in text style transfer.
Finetuning language models in the few-shot setting can considerably reduce the need for prompt engineering and achieve competitive accuracy with null prompts.
The proposed method enables arbitrary style transfer in real-time using a novel adaptive instance normalization layer.
A dual reinforcement learning framework will directly transfer the style of the text via a one-step mapping model, without any separation of content and style.
A zero-shot dual system, trained on English-French and English-Spanish, outperforms a standard Neural Machine Translation system in zero-shot translation performance on Spanish-French.
Content Transfer for long-form text generation will significantly improve the generation of contextually fitting sentences grounded in external textual sources compared to existing methods.
The proposed approach for zero-shot named entity recognition and classification will outperform baselines adapted from machine reading comprehension and zero-shot text classification.
Leveraging representations learned by a multilingual Neural Machine Translation system enables zero-shot multilinguality in other Natural Language Processing tasks.
Style-augmented translation models are able to capture the style variations of translators and to generate translations with different styles on new data.
Masking can be utilized as an efficient alternative to finetuning for pretrained language models.

given_idea: Enhancing the performance of KQA Pro in complex question answering over knowledge bases requires sustained research investment.
original: KQA Pro is challenging and Complex question answering over knowledge base requires further research efforts.

top_k.sentence: KQA Pro is a large-scale dataset for Complex Question Answering over knowledge bases that addresses the shortcomings of existing benchmarks by providing diverse instances and depicting reasoning processes.
KQA Pro is challenging and Complex question answering over knowledge base requires further research efforts.
ComQA dataset will drive future research on complex factoid question answering.
Complex Question Answering over Knowledge Base aims to automatically answer natural language questions via well-structured relation information between entities stored in knowledge bases.
Automatically extracting evidence from heterogeneous knowledge sources will improve commonsense question answering performance.

Knowledge-aided open-domain question answering (KAQA) method improves relevant document retrieval and candidate answer reranking by considering the relationship between a question and the documents and the relationship between candidate documents.
Enhancing key-value memory neural networks will improve reasoning abilities on complex questions in knowledge based question answering.
Incorporating side information from external knowledge bases improves the accuracy of the baseline question answering system.
The Knowledge Embedding based Question Answering (KEQA) framework outperforms the state-of-the-art question answering over knowledge graph methods.
Knowledge-Enriched Answer Generator (KEAG) improves answer quality over models without knowledge and existing knowledge-aware models.
The proposed semantic parsing framework for question answering using a knowledge base will outperform previous methods substantially.
Query rewriting with background knowledge will outperform several strong baselines on the ARC dataset in the end-to-end question answering task.
Using n-tuple assertions in a knowledge-based question-answering system will effectively answer complex questions with rich semantic constraints.
Incorporating additional commonsense knowledge into language model based approaches will improve question answering performance in commonsense question answering tasks.
The proposed algorithm improves question answering performance compared to Deep Q-Network and a strong information-retrieval baseline.
NEQA will improve answering quality over time and adapt to new domains in open-domain question answering over knowledge bases.
Incorporating additional commonsense knowledge into language model-based approaches will improve question-answering performance in commonsense question answering domains.
Evaluating semantic parsing-based question answering models will yield reasonable performance compared to a web-based question answering baseline.
A multi-strategy and multisource question answering system can effectively answer natural language questions.
Exploiting external knowledge from Wikipedia and increasing training data improves accuracy in multiple-choice question answering tasks.
A joint probabilistic model for answer sentence ranking and answer extraction will substantially outperform state-of-the-art systems in both tasks.
The Stable Sparse Reward based Programmer (SSRP) model outperforms state-of-the-art models in noisy settings for Complex Knowledge Base Question Answering (KBQA) tasks.
Decomposing complex questions into a sequence of simple questions improves performance in answering complex questions.
HybridQA can serve as a challenging benchmark to study question answering with heterogeneous information.
Retrieval Question-Answering (ReQA) will serve as a benchmark for evaluating large-scale sentence-level answer retrieval models.
The proposed pattern-revising procedure will mitigate the error propagation problem in Question Answering over Knowledge Bases.
Performing topic unit linking will improve knowledge base question answering by covering a wider range of units and refining answer candidates through a generation-and-scoring approach.
Attention-based injection seems to be a preferable choice for knowledge integration in commonsense question answering.
The knowledge-enabled visual question answering model will outperform traditional visual question answering and knowledge base-based question-answering methods on the text-KVQA dataset.
Empowering a relation extraction method with additional evidence from Wikipedia will improve the performance of knowledge-based question answering systems.
Adding linguistic and world knowledge axioms to the Indonesian Semantic Analysis-based Question Answering System will increase its inferential power and robustness.
Generating paraphrases of the input question will improve the performance of the semantic parser for open-domain question answering.
Improving the question generation model with question answering through incorporating additional QA-specific signal as the loss function will enhance performance.
Using enhanced lexical semantic models will significantly improve answer sentence selection performance in question answering systems.
Question-answer driven sentence encoding (QuASE) learns representations from question-answer data, improving performance on downstream natural language processing tasks.
Incorporating commonsense-based functions improves the baseline on question answering tasks that require commonsense reasoning.
TextRay answers complex questions using a novel decompose-execute-join approach and significantly outperforms existing knowledge-based question answering systems on complex questions.
Feature-driven question answering will improve the generation and recognition of indicative signals for answer patterns in natural language question answering.
The proposed method will achieve state-of-the-art performance for the sentence-level answer-selection task on the WikiQA and TREC-QA datasets.
Generating synthetic question answering corpora using models of question generation and answer extraction will improve performance on SQuAD2 and NQ.
The TREC question answering track significantly improves the performance of question answering systems compared to traditional text retrieval systems.
Joint question answering and question generation improve each other through a training framework that leverages their probabilistic correlation.
Extending existing strong question answering frameworks to include previous questions will improve the overall question-answering accuracy in open-domain question answering.
Integrating a logic prover into a Question Answering system boosts the performance of the QA system on TREC questions by 30%.
The novel algorithm for textbook question answering significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods.

Complex Imperative Program Induction from Terminal Rewards (CIPITR) solves complex knowledge base question-answering considerably more accurately than key-value memory networks and neural symbolic machines.
FreebaseQA is suitable for model training in factoid question answering tasks beyond simpler questions.
Leveraging natural language relations between answer choices will improve the performance of machine comprehension.
The modified staged query graph generation method achieves state of the art on three benchmark knowledge base question answering datasets.
Incorporating easy, yet diverse samples can further improve learning in question answering models.

given_idea: The MECT architecture leverages the unique characteristics of Chinese characters to boost the performance of NER tasks.
original: Multi-metadata embedding based Cross-Transformer (MECT) will improve the performance of Chinese Named Entity Recognition by fusing the structural information of Chinese characters.

top_k.sentence: Multi-metadata embedding based Cross-Transformer (MECT) will improve the performance of Chinese Named Entity Recognition by fusing the structural information of Chinese characters.
The component-enhanced Chinese character embedding models will demonstrate effectiveness in capturing semantic information for NLP tasks.
Glyph-based models are able to consistently outperform word/char ID-based models in a wide range of Chinese NLP tasks.
Incorporating the word lexicon into character representations will significantly improve the performance and inference speed of Chinese named entity recognition models.
Creating embeddings for characters based on their visual characteristics will improve processing of instances with rare characters in languages such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
The introduction of a Chinese corpus for fine-grained entity typing will enhance the performance of neural models on this task.

CNN based models that incorporate semantic information of Chinese characters will show an improvement over the baseline BERT-BiLSTM-CRF model for Named Entity Recognition.

Incorporating Chinese characters of words will improve lexical sememe prediction performance, especially for low-frequency words.

A character-based neural model for the joint task enhanced by multi-channel attention of n-grams will outperform strong baseline models and achieve state-of-the-art performance on Chinese word segmentation and part-of-speech tagging.

The neural network which combines object-level image information and character-level text information will outperform the state-of-the-art methods in named entity recognition.
The character-based bidirectional LSTM-CRF neural architecture will achieve better performance for Chinese Named Entity Recognition compared to systems requiring hand-crafted features.
Character-level Neural Machine Translation models will achieve promising results for Chinese-English translation by using the Wubi encoding scheme.
ChineseBERT incorporates both the glyph and pinyin information of Chinese characters into language model pretraining and yields significant performance boost over baseline models.

The model will learn Chinese word embeddings via three-level composition, demonstrating superior performance on word similarity, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and part-of-speech tagging.
The release of the Span-Extraction dataset for Chinese machine reading comprehension will further accelerate the Chinese machine reading comprehension research.
Phonological features outperform character-based models in various NER-related tasks.
The neural pinyin-to-character model with gated-attention mechanism will significantly improve user experience in Chinese input methods compared to traditional models.
Baidu neural machine translation systems based on the Transformer architecture with improvements will achieve high BLEU scores in Chinese<->English translation.

The Convolutional Attention Network (CAN) for Chinese Named Entity Recognition (NER) outperforms state-of-the-art methods without word embedding and external lexicon resources on different domain datasets.
Finetuning a subword model on characters will enable a character-level neural machine translation model that captures morphological phenomena and shows robustness to noise.
The proposed method, seg_tok, improves the performances of Chinese pre-trained language models on sentence level tasks and efficiency compared to character-based vocabulary.
Both characters and embeddings achieve good performance in scoring the outputs of machine translation systems.
The mixed character-word architecture will achieve substantial performance gains over baseline models on the sentence similarity task.

Glyph-aware embedding of Chinese characters will effectively incorporate the visual appearance of a character’s glyph in its representation, improving performance in language modeling and word segmentation tasks.

Charagram embeddings outperform more complex architectures based on character-level recurrent and convolutional neural networks, achieving new state-of-the-art performance on several similarity tasks.
Radical embedding achieves comparable, and sometimes even better, results than competing methods in Chinese language processing tasks.
Char-based models consistently outperform word-based models in deep learning-based Chinese Natural Language Processing tasks.
The ByteNet decoder attains state-of-the-art performance on character-level language modelling and outperforms the previous best results obtained with recurrent networks.
A previously unstudied combination of character trigram representations composed with bi-LSTMs outperforms most others in language modeling tasks.

NEZHA achieves state-of-the-art performances when finetuned on several representative Chinese tasks, including named entity recognition, sentence matching, Chinese sentiment classification, and natural language inference.
The character-level tagger for language-independent Named Entity Recognition will achieve close to state-of-the-art performance in seven languages using only labeled NER data.
Long Short-Term Memory units can learn to map character-level representations of computer programs to their correct outputs.
ZEN, a BERT-based Chinese text encoder enhanced by n-gram representations, will achieve state-of-the-art results on Chinese NLP tasks while requiring less resources than other published encoders.
The competition introduces a new dataset and tasks for reading Chinese text in natural images, highlighting the need for more future research on the Chinese text reading problem.
Multi-prototype Chinese character embeddings are significantly better than a single-prototype baseline for character similarity and improve the F-score in Chinese Named Entity Recognition tasks.
Character sequences as input are preferred for parsing Discourse Representation Structures in both English and Chinese.
Applying the self-attention mechanism to dependency parsing will lead to competitive performance on both English and Chinese benchmark data.
The neural Pinyin-to-character conversion model augmented by an online updated vocabulary outperforms commercial input method engines and state-of-the-art traditional models.
A novel technique for character detection will achieve significant improvements over state of the art on multiple datasets.
Bilingual character representation will effectively address out-of-vocabulary words in code-switching named entity recognition.
Applying transfer learning from a source English model results in an average Character Error Rate improvement of 5.99 ± 5.48 for twelve target languages.

A convolutional neural network (CNN)-based method that incorporates lexicons using a rethinking mechanism will achieve better performance and be faster than both word-level and character-level baseline methods for Chinese named entity recognition.
The character-level architecture for learning bidirectional retrieval models will outperform state-of-the-art approaches in description and image retrieval tasks.
Multi-layer Embedding with Memory Network (MEMEN) will achieve competitive results in precision and efficiency for machine comprehension tasks compared to existing methods.
The novel neural framework will achieve competitive or better performances compared to previous state-of-the-art methods for Chinese word segmentation.

A deep learning architecture that combines distributional information, morphological structure, psychological measures, corpus-counts, and topical information will achieve reasonable performance on the complex word identification task.
A combination of BERT, BPEmb, and character representations works best across languages and tasks.

given_idea: Large language models can accurately assess online content without relying on human-annotated data, ensuring more efficient evaluation processes.
original: An unreferenced automated evaluation metric using large pre-trained language models will achieve higher correlation with human annotations in an online setting without requiring true responses for comparison during inference.

top_k.sentence: An unreferenced automated evaluation metric using large pre-trained language models will achieve higher correlation with human annotations in an online setting without requiring true responses for comparison during inference.

The proposed method for content selection achieves a substantial improvement over context-agnostic methods in natural language generation.
Tokenizer-free language models with sufficient capacity can achieve competitive performance on large scale datasets.
Reference-less grammaticality metrics correlate very strongly with human judgments and are competitive with the leading reference-based evaluation metrics.
The automatic evaluation model based on conversational context will outperform existing evaluation metrics in terms of high correlation with human annotation scores.
Dataless hierarchical text classification can accurately categorize documents by understanding the labels of the sought-after categories without requiring labeled data.
Automatic evaluation of single topics and whole topic models will improve the intrinsic quality assessment of topic models.

Language-agnostic retrieval is a substantively new kind of cross-lingual evaluation.
A neural, end-to-end model for jointly extracting entities and their relations will match or exceed state-of-the-art performance without relying on external natural language processing tools.
