top_k.sentence: The method obtains good results with respect to a reference ranking produced by human raters.
The proposed method for transforming offensive comments into non-offensive ones will outperform previous style transfer models in both automatic metrics and human evaluations.

The proposed scoring method without fine-tuning produces strong baselines comparable to supervised approaches and provides a more stable training phase across random restarts with less annotated data.
Test statistics can reliably detect adversarially manipulated examples under specific conditions.
The USL-H score achieves good correlations with human judgment and maintains its configurability towards different aspects and metrics.
The system achieves very high reliability on unseen test data from the corpus.
The proposed approach to automatically determine which and how many sources should be exploited will result in performance increases of up to 24 F1 points.
The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator and its scales yielded scores with strong internal consistency and test-retest reliability estimates.
Best–worst scaling produces significantly more reliable results than the rating scale with the same total number of annotations.
RankME significantly improves the reliability and consistency of human ratings compared to traditional evaluation methods.
The controlled-production vocabulary-levels test was found to be reliable, valid, and practical.
The orthogonality approach is better at debiasing the model while maintaining competitive overall accuracy.
The proposed method combined with additional noises and the inconsistency strategy will effectively detect adversarial examples with a high detection success rate.
Resolution and reliability have a positive effect on the score in probabilistic forecasting.
FNNC achieves fairness in classification while maintaining high accuracy.
The method achieves an F1-score of 80.8%, exceeding the best baseline by almost 33 percentage points.
The proposed evaluation techniques will produce summary scores that replicate human assessments accurately without relying on gold-standard human summaries.
The model is reliable for a number of error types and style transfer aspects.
Kendall's tau correlates reliably with human ratings and reading times.
Different approaches and models for offensive tweet classification will be evaluated for their effectiveness in the OffensEval 2019 competition.
Estimators designed for cardinal scores with arbitrary miscalibrations will strictly and uniformly outperform all possible estimators that rely only on ranking.
A system combination of Support Vector Machines and Deep Neural Networks achieved the best results on offensive language detection, ranking 1st with an F1-score of 90.51% on the test set.
Simple methods typically yield performance almost as good as more sophisticated methods in supervised classification.
Perception Score achieves state-of-the-art results on all the tasks consistently in terms of correlation with human evaluation scores.
The system achieved good results for both the JJ and CC tasks, and the portable system determined relevant terms robustly.
The graph-enhanced model equipped with an attention-based matching module is able to achieve a much higher recall score while maintaining a high-level precision.
The statistical model that aggregates many manual pairwise comparisons will result in highly interpretable estimates and be less affected by noisy judges than previously proposed methods.
The marker-card technique has given us a reliable alternative to Q-sort for scaling conflict.
The method achieves high precision and recall and outperforms commercial machine translation systems.
Electric performs well when transferred to downstream tasks and is particularly effective at producing likelihood scores for text.
Both manipulation strategies are able to generate C-tests with the desired difficulty level.
The proposed approach achieves promising performance compared with other baseline methods.
The proposed method can provide reasonable explanations for predictions and outperform existing approaches.
Humans are able to correctly classify responses at a rate much better than chance, thus confirming that the task is feasible.
Automated scoring systems are susceptible to gaming behavior, and a framework is proposed for evaluating this susceptibility.
New measures and mechanisms will quantify and mitigate unfairness from position bias in rankings, achieving amortized individual fairness while retaining high ranking quality.
A robust methodology can distinguish bullies and aggressors from normal Twitter users with over 90% accuracy and AUC.
The new random-walk-based measure outperforms existing ones in capturing the intuitive notion of controversy.
The alignment method outperforms state-of-the-art systems developed for the same task.

The Directional Scoring Method (DSM) returns a ranked list of possible responses, and DSM’s top-ranked response is significantly more likely to be correct than the top-ranked responses of naive analogy estimations.
The proposed methodology provides always valid p-values and confidence intervals that are robust to continuous monitoring during A/B testing.
The category based method performs best among three grouping methods and outperforms all the baselines.
The novel unsupervised algorithm achieves significant improvement on recall compared to the previous method while maintaining approximately the same precision.
The weighted majority algorithm is shown to be robust with respect to errors in the data.
HeidelTime achieved the highest F-Score (86%) for the extraction and the best results in assigning the correct value attribute.

The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on two public datasets in different languages (Chinese and English), outperforming a number of competitive baselines by at least 2.08% in F-measure.
The automatic evaluation metric developed in this paper will provide a consistent estimator of fluency, outperforming individual parser metrics.
The new reliability coefficient provides researchers with information regarding the degree to which data may be trusted.
LINDEN significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of accuracy.
The proposed measures credit information retrieval methods for their ability to retrieve highly relevant documents and allow testing of statistical significance of effectiveness differences.

given_idea: Regularization strategies, namely dropout techniques for Mixture of Experts layers, Conditional MiE Routing, and Curriculum Learning methods, will prevent over-fitting and improve the performance of Mixture of Experts models on low-resource tasks.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Learning dropout patterns will improve the regularization of deep networks compared to conventional methods.
Gating Dropout improves a state-of-the-art Mixture of Experts model with faster wall-clock time convergence rates and better BLEU scores for a variety of model sizes and datasets.
Hash layers in large sparse models will outperform or be competitive with learning-to-route mixture-of-expert methods such as Switch Transformers and BASE Layers.
The Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts layer achieves greater than 1000x improvements in model capacity with only minor losses in computational efficiency.
Regularization techniques such as dropout and L2-regularization will reduce overfitting and improve transfer learning in neural machine translation.
Adopting regularizers that promote structured sparsity will improve performance, compactness, and model interpretability in structured prediction tasks.
The proposed regularization method can largely mitigate the representation degeneration problem and achieve better performance than baseline algorithms.

Mixture of Experts layers (MoEs) enable efficient scaling of language models through conditional computation, and MoEs can match the performance of dense models using approximately 4 times less compute.

Using a fixed dropout probability during training is a suboptimal choice, and an adaptive regularization scheme that smoothly increases the difficulty of the optimization problem leads to better generalization.
The parameter-efficient Mixture-of-Experts architecture will improve performance and efficiency of pre-trained language models while reducing total parameters compared to existing models.
Multi-gate Mixture-of-Experts (MMoE) will perform better than baseline methods when the tasks are less related.
The proposed regularization technique allows the learned representation to be decomposable into parts, requiring fewer examples to learn classifiers for novel categories.
Variational dropout, a generalization of Gaussian dropout where the dropout rates are learned, often leads to better models.

The Switch Transformer simplifies the Mixture of Experts routing algorithm and enables the training of large sparse models with lower precision formats, achieving significant improvements in pre-training speed and scalability.

Heterogeneous mixture-of-experts employing an expert choice method improves training convergence time by more than 2x and demonstrates higher performance in fine-tuning tasks compared to prior methods.
Introducing sparse regularization strategy can significantly improve the performance of commonly-used loss functions in the presence of noisy labels and class imbalance.
Curriculum learning exhibits robustness against unfavorable conditions such as strong regularization.
Mixture of Expert Clusters will improve performance on machine translation and natural language understanding tasks while mitigating overfitting and sparse data allocation problems.
Propagation regularizer can achieve efficient and effective learning with extremely scarce labeled samples by suppressing confirmation bias.
The boosted mixture of experts ensemble learning scheme will improve classification and regression performance compared to classical ensemble approaches.
A dense student model (OneS) can achieve performance comparable to a sparse mixture-of-experts model while being more hardware-friendly and efficient.
Algorithmic approaches can find better results for hyper-parameter optimization in training neural networks and deep belief networks.
Adding a regularization that preserves pretraining weights is effective in mitigating the destructive tendency of few-shot finetuning.

l1/l∞-regularization yields improved statistical efficiency if the overlap parameter is large enough (α > 2/3), but performs worse than a naive Lasso-based approach for moderate to small overlap (α < 2/3).
Mixout regularizes learning to minimize the deviation from one of the two models and increases the stability of finetuning and average accuracy when applied to BERT on downstream tasks in GLUE.
Route constrained optimization (RCO) greatly reduces the lower bound of congruence loss for knowledge distillation and improves performance on close-set classification tasks.

An extension of Spectral Networks incorporating a Graph Estimation procedure will match or improve performance on large-scale classification problems compared to Dropout Networks with fewer parameters to estimate.

Injecting noise to hidden units during training optimizes the lower bound of the true objective and a technique to achieve a tighter lower bound using multiple noise samples per training example will improve generalization performance.
Random dropout improves performance of neural networks by preventing co-adaptation of feature detectors.
Sparsity-inducing regularizers during training of Convolution Neural Networks will result in dramatic reductions in memory requirements with minimal loss in accuracy.
Vision Mixture of Experts (V-MoE) matches the performance of state-of-the-art networks while requiring as little as half of the compute at inference time.
Denoising autoencoders can be effectively trained to produce robust representations that improve pattern classification performance.
The new variational inference based dropout technique will improve performance in recurrent neural networks, specifically in language modelling and sentiment analysis tasks.
A learned conditional masking mechanism enables the model to strongly generalize far outside of its training range with near-perfect accuracy on a variety of algorithms.
Zoneout will improve the generalization of recurrent neural networks by stochastically preserving hidden activations compared to traditional dropout methods.
Dropout-based uncertainty-driven self-training technique (DUST) will lead to substantially improved automatic speech recognition results compared to self-training without filtering.
Dropout training in deep neural networks will improve model uncertainty representation without sacrificing computational complexity or test accuracy.
Residual-guided knowledge distillation will significantly reduce the performance gap between a heavy teacher network and a lightweight student network.
Churn is indeed an issue for standard image classification tasks, and using minimum entropy regularizers and a novel variant of co-distillation can reduce churn while improving model accuracy.
A systematic, unifying taxonomy categorizes existing regularization methods in deep learning.
Dropout regularizer is first-order equivalent to an L2 regularizer applied after scaling the features by an estimate of the inverse diagonal Fisher information matrix.
FastMoE will optimize the training speed of Mixture-of-Expert models in a distributed system based on PyTorch.

Replacing saturating activation functions with noisy variants helps training in many contexts, yielding state-of-the-art or competitive results on different datasets and tasks, especially when curriculum learning is necessary.
Masking can be utilized as an efficient alternative to finetuning for pretrained language models.

Gradient descent training with regularization by the distance between the network parameters to initialization leads to a generalization guarantee on the clean data distribution despite being trained using noisy labels.
DeepSpeed-MoE provides an end-to-end Mixture-of-Experts training and inference solution that offers 7.3x better latency and cost compared to existing Mixture-of-Experts inference solutions.
Mixture of Expert/Imitator Networks will improve the performance of deep neural networks in text classification tasks by utilizing unlabeled data.
DEMix layers reduce test-time perplexity, increase training efficiency, and enable rapid adaptation in language models.
Penalizing low entropy output distributions acts as a strong regularizer in supervised learning.
Increasing the number of training examples can alleviate local optima issues in Expectation-Maximization algorithms.

given_idea: MM-SHAP reliably quantifies in which proportions a multimodal model uses individual modalities.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: DIME enables accurate and fine-grained analysis of multimodal models while maintaining generality across arbitrary modalities, model architectures, and tasks.
Multimodal Dynamics will provide trustworthy multimodal fusion by dynamically evaluating both feature-level and modality-level informativeness for different samples.
Multimodal machine learning aims to build models that can process and relate information from multiple modalities.
The empirical multimodally-additive function projection (EMAP) isolates whether cross-modal interactions improve performance for a given model on a given task.

Multimodal Fusion approach for learning modality-Specific and modality-Agnostic representations (MFSA) will refine multimodal representations and leverage the complementarity across different modalities.
Automatic multi-modal summarization (MMS) is essential for obtaining key information from multiple forms of representations.
Modality-Specific Learning Rate (MSLR) method will outperform global learning rates on multiple tasks and settings in late-fusion multimodal models.

The proposed method can successfully measure visual knowledge transfer capabilities in models and the novel model architecture shows promising results for leveraging multimodal knowledge in a unimodal setting.

Multimodal word distributions formed from Gaussian mixtures will capture uniquely expressive semantic information and outperform alternatives, such as word2vec skip-grams and Gaussian embeddings, on benchmark datasets.
The proposed multimodal representation learning framework will effectively learn associations between data modalities by minimizing the variation of information.

given_idea: An adversary can inject a backdoor in neural code search models, which return buggy or even vulnerable code with security/privacy issues.

This paper conducts a comprehensive review of backdoor attacks and defenses in the field of natural language processing.
Label-consistent backdoor attacks can be executed efficiently by leveraging adversarial perturbations and generative models.
BadPre is the first task-agnostic backdoor attack against pre-trained Natural Language Processing models.
Black-box neural models of CLEVR can be easily fooled by an adversarial player, raising doubts about their reasoning capabilities.
The novel form of backdoor attack can fool the model by pasting the trigger at random locations on unseen images, although the model performs well on clean data.

Adversarial and backdoor attacks based on text style transfer will achieve attack success rates exceeding 90% on popular NLP models.
Malicious primitive models pose immense threats to the security of machine learning systems.
Current state-of-the-art models are vulnerable to simple black-box attacks in machine reading comprehension.

The proposed weight-poisoning attack method introduces a layerwise weight poisoning strategy to plant deeper backdoors and a combinatorial trigger that cannot be easily detected.

Existing image backdoor attacks are far less effective on videos under certain conditions, and a universal adversarial trigger can manipulate state-of-the-art video models with high success rates.

Separating disambiguation from composition in distributional semantics will produce better composite representations.

Distributional inference improves sparse word representations on several word similarity benchmarks and is competitive with the state-of-the-art for adjective-noun, noun-noun, and verb-object compositions while being fully interpretable.
The new learning method for tensors will outperform existing leading methods in compositional distributional semantics.
The evaluation of Compositional Distributional Semantic Models on full sentences will predict human judgments on semantic relatedness and entailment.
A factorized compositional distributional semantics model for transitive verb constructions will outperform recent previous work on established tasks.
The composition function based models perform slightly better than the constituent based models for compositionality detection in compound nouns.
Separating syntactic from semantic learning may be a useful heuristic for capturing compositional structure.
The hierarchical generative probabilistic model of topical phrases will find substantially better, more interpretable topical phrases than competing models.

The hierarchical generative probabilistic model incorporating both n-gram statistics and latent topic variables exhibits better predictive accuracy than either a hierarchical Dirichlet bigram language model or a unigram topic model.
Using word embeddings to predict the compositionality of multiword expressions will outperform count-based distributional similarity methods.
The combination of an unsupervised distributional word space model with a supervised maximum-entropy classification model will effectively induce appropriate paraphrases for noun compounds.
Distributional semantic models (DSMs) will predict the degree of semantic compositionality of nominal compounds with high correlation to human judgments.
Injecting subword-level information into semantic word vectors will facilitate word-level prediction in morphologically-rich languages.
The matrix-space model for semantic composition will show statistically significant improvements in performance over a bag-of-words model in the context of phrase-level sentiment analysis.
Novel denotational similarity metrics will be at least as beneficial as distributional similarities for tasks that require semantic inference.
A new dependency-based Distributional Semantic Model will capture specifics of the relations between words and contexts, leading to good performance on both intrinsic and extrinsic evaluation tasks.
The tensor-based factorization model for semantic compositionality exceeds the state of the art in a similarity task for transitive phrases.
The novel unsupervised approach to detecting the compositionality of multi-word expressions will achieve state-of-the-art performance on a verb-object dataset of human compositionality ratings.
Modeling metaphor as linear transformations in a vector space improves semantic representations in compositional distributional semantic models.
Exploiting distributional similarity information during training can improve visual word predictors for certain words, such as entry-level nouns or hypernyms.
Learning composition models for phrase embeddings will improve performance on language modeling and phrase semantic similarity tasks.
Distributional information can be used to predict the diachronic direction of conversion for homophonous noun-verb pairs.
The compositional language model using Predicate-Argument Structures will improve upon the state of the art by as much as ∼10% in relative performance on the subject-verb-object dataset.
Distributional semantics will foster greater cross-fertilization of theoretical and computational approaches to language.
A method that integrates compositional semantics into learning performs better than all other alternatives.

The grammatical type specific model improves composition flexibility by adopting vector-tensor-vector operations and outperforms state-of-the-art composition methods in the task of computing phrase similarities.
Integrating multiplicative features into supervised distributional methods will boost the performance of these methods in lexical entailment.
A novel taxonomy of relations will improve the automatic interpretation of noun compounds in natural language processing applications.
A generative distributional model for the unsupervised induction of natural language syntax will produce higher quality analyses than previously exhibited by unsupervised systems.
A method of canonicalizing deverbal noun representations will improve knowledge-representation systems.
The polynomial projection function can learn semantic composition and identify non-compositionality in an unsupervised way, outperforming all other baselines.

Combining ConceptNet with word embeddings acquired from distributional semantics will improve natural language applications by providing understanding that is not achievable from distributional semantics alone.
The proposed method (CNNSE) allows word and phrase vectors to adapt to the notion of composition and provides an interpretable vector space model for semantics.
A simple discriminative learning framework will effectively define statistical models that learn compositional semantic theories from corpora and databases.
Constructing interpretable word vectors from hand-crafted linguistic resources will yield competitive performance compared to standard distributional approaches.
The recursive neural network model will learn compositional vector representations for phrases and sentences of arbitrary syntactic type and length.

Computing distributed meaning representations for discourse arguments by composition up the syntactic parse tree and performing a downward compositional pass to capture the meaning of coreferent entity mentions will significantly improve the prediction of implicit discourse relations.
Low-rank tensors can match the performance of full tensors while using significantly fewer parameters in compositional distributional semantics.
Incorporating prior knowledge from semantic resources will improve lexical semantic embeddings compared to embeddings learned solely on raw text.

Integrating compositional morphological representations into a vector-based probabilistic language model will lead to substantial reductions in perplexity and improvements in translation performance for morphologically rich languages.
A structured generative latent variable model that integrates information from multiple contextual representations will achieve state-of-the-art results on Word Sense Induction datasets.
Compositional character models will yield state-of-the-art results in language modeling and part-of-speech tagging compared to traditional word representation models.
Semantic content can be computed using the Kullback-Leibler divergence.
A hierarchical annotation scheme for semantic parsing will allow the representation of compositional queries and can be efficiently and accurately parsed by standard constituency parsing models.
Shallow approaches are as good as more computationally intensive alternatives for modeling compositional meaning in terms of phrase similarity and paraphrase detection.

given_idea: ConvSumX is more faithful towards input text compared to existing cross-lingual summarization corpora.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Jointly learning to align and summarize will ease the cross-lingual summarization training and outperform competitive models.
The ConvSumX Challenge will motivate researchers to investigate solutions that integrate conversation summarization and machine translation.
A hierarchical model for cross-lingual summarization will generate better summaries than comparison models in the few-shot setting.
MultiSumm model will significantly outperform a multi-lingual baseline model in multilingual abstractive summarization.
The proposed cross-lingual summarisation corpus will facilitate the analysis of cross-lingual summarisation tasks and improve the performance of multi-lingual pre-trained models.
State-of-the-art summarization models perform poorly on the Multi-LexSum task despite high-quality summaries in the training data.
The comparative summarization framework CoCoSum can produce higher-quality contrastive and common summaries than state-of-the-art opinion summarization models.
This survey presents the first systematic critical review on the datasets, approaches, and challenges in cross-lingual summarization.
The proposed abstractive cross-language summarization framework outperforms the state-of-the-art extractive systems in both automatic and manual evaluations.
SummaCConv enables natural language inference models to be successfully used for inconsistency detection by segmenting documents into sentence units and aggregating scores between pairs of sentences.
Unicoder will improve cross-lingual natural language processing tasks compared to existing models.
XL-Sum induces competitive results compared to the ones obtained using similar monolingual datasets.

The robust neural abstractive summarization system for cross-lingual summarization achieves significantly higher fluency than a standard copy-attention summarizer on automatically translated input documents.
Enhancing factual robustness will improve the faithfulness of Seq2Seq summarization models.
Mixed-lingual pre-training will effectively boost the performance of cross-lingual summarization.
A novel Knowledge-Distillation-based framework for Cross-Lingual Summarization will outperform state-of-the-art models under both high and low-resourced settings.
Cross-lingual XMEANT outperforms monolingual MEANT by replacing the monolingual context vector model in MEANT with simple translation probabilities and incorporating bracketing ITG constraints.

Neural Cross-Lingual Summarization (NCLS) achieves remarkable improvement over traditional pipeline methods on both English-to-Chinese and Chinese-to-English cross-lingual summarization human-corrected test sets.
Using the Wasserstein barycenter as a mean language will improve multilingual alignment quality compared to using a poorly chosen pivot language.
Combining static and contextual multilingual embeddings will improve multilingual representations and align the representation space of XLM-R.
A new unsupervised method for aligning text based on Doc2Vec embeddings will effectively create a monolingual parallel corpus for text simplification.
Ernie-M aligns the representation of multiple languages with monolingual corpora to enhance the performance of cross-lingual models.
Translated texts yield better language models for statistical machine translation than original texts.
The proposed method can significantly outperform the baselines in cross-lingual summarization tasks.
The proposed method for direct cross-lingual summarization significantly outperforms the baseline approaches while being more cost efficient during inference.
Different approaches of constructing cross-lingual summarization datasets will lead to different degrees of translationese.
A_universal_cross-language_representation_leads_to_better_multilingual_translation_performance.
SummaRuNNer achieves performance better than or comparable to state-of-the-art in extractive summarization of documents.
The contrastive aligned joint learning model for multilingual summarization (CALMS) achieves significant improvement over monolingual models in all languages.
Incorporating topic information into the convolutional sequence-to-sequence model will improve coherence, diversity, and informativeness of generated summaries.
High quality source-reference alignment algorithms will improve the performance of multi-document summarization tasks.

An unsupervised method that automatically builds the monolingual parallel corpus for text simplification using sentence similarity based on word embeddings will outperform existing methods that require human annotation.
Concatenated power mean word embeddings will substantially outperform complex techniques such as InferSent cross-lingually.
Multiplicative models are superior to additive alternatives in representing the meaning of phrases and sentences in vector space.
Extractive summarizers are in general better than their abstractive counterparts thanks to strength in faithfulness and factual-consistency.
Highlight-based Reference-less Evaluation of Summarization (HighRES) improves inter-annotator agreement in comparison to using the source document directly.
TCSum leverages plentiful text classification data to improve the performance of multi-document summarization.
The Multi-Task framework for Cross-Lingual Abstractive Summarization (MCLAS) significantly outperforms three baseline models in both low-resource and full-dataset scenarios.
The plug-and-play module RelAttn effectively improves summarizers and outperforms existing methods in both zero- and few-shot settings.
XLM-E outperforms baseline models on various cross-lingual understanding tasks with much less computation cost.
AREDSUM-CTX achieves significantly better performance than AREDSUM-SEQ and state-of-the-art extractive summarization baselines by modeling diversity explicitly in a separate step.
Detecting content-heavy sentences in cross-lingual context will improve the understandability of translations for English speakers.
Agreement-oriented multi-document summarization will improve article-summary and cluster-summary entailment in generated summaries compared to existing baselines.
Prompt-tuning achieves much better cross-lingual transfer than fine-tuning across datasets.
A cascade architecture for neural text summarization will perform comparably to or better than end-to-end systems in terms of content selection and coherence.
L’AMBRE will effectively evaluate the morphosyntactic well-formedness of generated texts in multilingual settings.
Using bilingual information from both the source and translated documents will improve cross-language document summarization.
Using existing summaries as soft templates will improve the stability and readability of generated summaries in seq2seq summarization systems.
Word-Exchange Aligning Model (WEAM) can significantly improve the zero-shot cross-lingual transfer performance.
SMART metric outperforms all competing metrics on the SummEval summarization meta-evaluation dataset.

given_idea: Self-Contrastive Learning (SSCL) will alleviate the over-smoothing problem in pre-trained language models, leading to improved unsupervised sentence representations.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Contrastive self-supervised training objectives will improve language modeling, zero to few-shot learning, and pretraining data-efficiency in natural language processing.
Contrastive self-supervised learning at the sentence level will improve language understanding compared to existing token-based pretraining approaches.
Self-guided contrastive learning method will improve the quality of BERT sentence representations compared to competitive baselines.
Supervised contrastive learning objective for fine-tuning will improve generalization and robustness of pre-trained language models compared to cross-entropy loss.
A novel generative self-supervised learning objective based on phrase reconstruction will achieve better performance in sentence representation learning compared to current contrastive methods.
Deep Contrastive Learning for Unsupervised Textual Representations will close the performance gap between unsupervised and supervised pretraining for universal sentence encoders.
Peer-Contrastive Learning (PCL) with diverse augmentations will improve the quality of unsupervised sentence embeddings compared to existing mono-augmenting strategies.
Multi-level supervised contrastive learning framework named MultiSCL will improve accuracy in low-resource natural language inference compared to existing models.

The contrastive self-training framework, COSINE, will enable fine-tuning language models with weak supervision, outperforming the strongest baseline and achieving competitive performance with fully-supervised fine-tuning methods.
Pair-level Supervised Contrastive Learning approach (PairSCL) will outperform existing methods in natural language inference tasks by improving accuracy on sentence pair representations.
Debiased Contrastive Learning of unsupervised sentence Representations will alleviate the influence of improper negatives and improve the uniformity of the representation space.
The self-training technique for language model fine-tuning will outperform other state-of-the-art supervised and semi-supervised counterparts on sentence classification tasks.
Graph self-supervised training will improve the structure awareness of pre-trained language models over abstract meaning representation graphs.

The self-supervised contrastive learning framework will model discriminative semantic features of both in-domain intents and out-of-domain intents from unlabeled data, improving efficiency and robustness.
Self-training pre-trained language models in zero- and few-shot scenarios will improve performance on data-scarce varieties using only resources from data-rich ones.

The composite baseline made of self-supervised contrastive representation learning, clustering, and language modeling shows better than chance performance on all four metrics for spoken language modeling from raw speech.
Self-evolution learning for pretrained language models will effectively extract knowledge from input pretraining data and improve performance on downstream tasks.
Self-training with pre-trained language models can significantly improve neural model performance in low-resource settings by utilizing limited task-specific labels and rationales.
Large Language Models can self-improve with only unlabeled datasets.

Unsupervised automatic speech recognition can improve the representations from speech self-supervised models and serves as an efficient connector between speech and textual pre-trained models, enhancing performance on various spoken language understanding tasks.
Smoothed contrastive sentence embedding (IS-CSE) will improve generalization performance in unsupervised sentence embeddings compared to instance-level embeddings.
Virtual augmentation Supported Contrastive Learning of sentence representations (VaSCL) sets a new state-of-the-art for unsupervised sentence representation learning.
Self-supervised contrastive representation learning will improve the performance of spoken question answering tasks.
Self-Contrastive Decorrelation (SCD) achieves comparable results with state-of-the-art methods on multiple benchmarks without using contrastive pairs.

A fast and simple algorithm for training neural probabilistic language models based on noise-contrastive estimation reduces training times by more than an order of magnitude without affecting the quality of the resulting models.
Triple contrastive learning (TCL) will improve vision-language representation learning by leveraging both cross-modal and intra-modal self-supervision.
Contrastive learning with Augmented and Retrieved Data for Sentence embedding (CARDS) significantly surpasses the current state-of-the-art on STS benchmarks in the unsupervised setting.
Contrastive masked language modeling (CMLM) surpasses several recent post-training methods in few-shot settings without the need for data augmentation.
Differentiable data augmentation during contrastive learning will yield significant improvements over existing methods for sentence representation learning.
The information minimization based contrastive learning model InforMin-CL will retain useful information and discard redundant information in unsupervised sentence representation learning.
An unsupervised framework based on self-talk will improve the performance of zero-shot language model baselines on commonsense tasks.
Incorporating syntactic structure into neural language models will improve language modeling performance as evidenced by lower perplexity and better quality parse trees.
Multilingual contrastive pretraining significantly enhances sentence representations, yielding a large performance gain on both benchmarks.

Contrastive Learning for Sentence Representation (CLEAR) employs multiple sentence-level augmentation strategies to learn a noise-invariant sentence representation and outperforms existing methods on SentEval and GLUE benchmarks.
Pretrained language models can generate high-quality sentence embeddings without the need for labeled data, finetuning, or modifications to the pretraining objective.
Contrastive learning on English data can learn high-quality universal cross-lingual sentence embeddings without any parallel data.

The proposed framework for learning sentence representations will outperform state-of-the-art unsupervised and supervised representation learning methods on several downstream natural language processing tasks while achieving an order of magnitude speedup in training time.
Weakly supervised contrastive learning framework (WCL) improves the quality of self-supervised representations across different datasets.
The reproduction of the robust self-learning method for fully unsupervised cross-lingual mappings of word embeddings is feasible with some minor assumptions.

Adapting large-scale pretrained language models to learn text representations and proposing a stochastic sampling scheme will lead to improved generalization and a new state-of-the-art performance in vision-and-language navigation.
Multi-task self-supervised learning will achieve competitive performance in disfluency detection using less than 1% of the training data compared to previous systems.
Self-training a self-attentive model sets a new state-of-the-art for disfluency detection, providing benefits orthogonal to pre-trained contextualized word representations.

A novel dataset-internal contrastive autoencoding approach to self-supervised pretraining will demonstrate marked improvements in zero-shot, few-shot, and solely supervised learning performance, even under an unfavorable low-resource scenario.

Self-supervised contrastive learning approach will significantly reduce the required number of annotations while improving generalization on unseen skills in user satisfaction prediction for conversational agents.
Pairwise supervised contrastive learning of sentence representations will outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in understanding sentence semantics.
Token-level contrastive distillation will improve the quality of word embeddings in generative pre-trained language models compared to previous quantization methods.
Deep Bregman divergence will enhance contrastive loss in self-supervised learning, improving the quality of learned representations compared to conventional methods.
SSMBA will improve out-of-domain robustness in natural language models compared to existing data augmentation methods.

The robust self-learning method for fully unsupervised cross-lingual mappings of word embeddings succeeds in all tested scenarios and obtains the best published results in standard datasets, even surpassing previous supervised systems.
FreeLB enhances the generalization of language models by promoting higher invariance in the embedding space through adversarial perturbations.

given_idea: A bias affects human naming choices for people playing sports, with speakers producing names indicating the sport more often for men or boys than for women or girls.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The collective concept person/people is not gender-neutral but rather prioritizes men over women.
Named entity recognition models exhibit gender bias, as relatively more female names are not recognized as PERSON entities compared to male names.
Gender prejudice occurs more in gendered rather than genderless languages.
Adapters introduce a new type of bias where words become more similar to female- than male pronouns.
Unisex names are given with substantial frequency to both genders, with a change in preference from males to females over the years in Pennsylvania.
The level of concern is dependent on gender, with females being more likely to express concern than males.
Gender bias in word embeddings from Wikipedia has developed over time, with art-related words becoming more female biased and career-related words more strongly associated with male than female.
The assignment of genders to nouns is not entirely arbitrary but may to some extent reflect the perceived masculine or feminine properties of the nouns’ referents.
Gender fair language would reduce the impact of the gender connotation on participants’ perceptions.
Larger capacity models tend to exhibit more gender bias and greater stereotyping of occupations by gender.
Identifying as a woman is associated with higher rates of support but also higher rates of disparagement.
There exists gender bias in the dataset and such bias is amplified by the embeddings.
The gender of one conversation side influences lexical use of the other side.
Biased data in Artificial Intelligence-Natural Language Processing models amplifies gender bias, resulting in the underrepresentation of the female gender in various fields.
Significant linguistic differences exist across individuals with different gender and stance in expressing opinions about abortion.
Entrainment is more important to the perception of female-male social behavior than it is for same-gender pairs.
Social biases exist in large English debate portals, with debate corpora containing unbalanced and biased data, mostly in favor of male people with European-American names.
Gender is a social construct that varies across cultures.
Gender bias in Natural Language Processing systems varies across different human languages.
The degree to which gender bias differs with the corpora used for training can significantly impact the bias learned by Natural Language Processing systems.
Gender and racial biases are interdependent, and their intersection triggers distinct effects.
A taxonomy is proposed for structural and contextual gender biases which can manifest themselves in text.
The categorization of gender in bibliographic analyses introduces biases that affect research productivity and scientific impact.
The positivity bias applies to nouns and verbs as well as adjectives and is moderated by personality traits and gender.
Gender bias is encoded strongly in contextual embeddings representing professions and stereotypical nouns.
A language-model-based approach will quantify differences in questions posed to female vs. male athletes in sports journalism.
Sexist people are less likely to detect and report gender biases in image search results.
Adolescent girls are required to navigate gender constructs and sexuality stereotypes in traditionally masculine sports.
Women used more words related to psychological and social processes, while men referred more to object properties and impersonal topics.
Using word association tests will provide a different perspective on gender stereotypes compared to word-embedding-based bias scores.
Sex bias against female participants in clinical studies persists, but results differ when studies vs participants are the measurement units.
There are biases in lexical sentiment analysis accuracy between reviews authored by males and females.
Several of the sentiment analysis systems show statistically significant bias towards certain races and genders.
Dialogue systems show significant prejudice towards different genders and races.
There are differences in characterization and structure between women and men in Wikipedia biographies, some of which can be attributed to gender bias in Wikipedia content.
Speakers tend to use pronouns more often for goal entities than source entities.
Extending the challenge set to include gender-biased adjectives and verbs will reveal a significant influence of these elements on gender bias in machine translation systems.
Topical gender bias interacts with grammatical gender associations, which are surpassed in magnitude, and both may be attenuated by corpus lemmatization.
The varying levels of regard towards different demographics serve as a defining metric for bias in natural language generation.
Gender stereotypes in education contribute to the perpetuation of gender differences and limit action repertoires for both sexes.
Grammatical gender affects word representations of inanimate nouns, resulting in nouns with the same gender being closer to each other than nouns with different gender.
Women use many words from the joy-sadness axis, whereas men prefer terms from the fear-trust axis.
Segmenting methods influence the ability to translate gender and certain segmentation approaches penalize the representation of feminine linguistic markings.
Grammatically masculine role-nouns in German favor a male-biased interpretation rather than a gender-neutral interpretation.
Wikipedia pages tend to intermingle personal life events with professional events for females but not for males.
Gender-fair language will contribute to the reduction of gender stereotyping and discrimination.
The metrics skew and stereotype quantify and analyze the gender bias present in contextual language models, and there is a trade-off between these two forms of bias.
There may be a gender bias in library and information science publishing, even though female authors have become more numerous.
Audio will provide additional information to reduce gender bias in speech translation.
Responses to women are more likely to be emotive and about the speaker as an individual rather than about the content being responded to.

given_idea: InfoMetIC achieves significantly better correlation with human judgements than existing metrics on multiple benchmarks.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Data-QuestEval metric obtains state-of-the-art correlations with human judgment on the WebNLG and WikiBio benchmarks.
Flexible semi-automatic to automatic summary evaluation metrics will improve human-metric correlations compared to existing metrics.
BERTScore correlates better with human judgments and provides stronger model selection performance than existing metrics.
RankME significantly improves the reliability and consistency of human ratings compared to traditional evaluation methods.
The proposed evaluator achieves a strong correlation (> 0.6) with human judgement and generalizes robustly to diverse responses and corpora.
InfoLM achieves statistically significant improvement and two figure correlation gains in many configurations compared to existing metrics on both summarization and data2text generation tasks.
The uniformly designed metrics achieve stronger or comparable correlations with human judgement compared to state-of-the-art metrics in each of diverse tasks.
New combinations of components outperform existing measures with respect to correlation to human ratings.

QuestEval substantially improves the correlation with human judgments over four evaluation dimensions (consistency, coherence, fluency, and relevance) compared to established metrics such as ROUGE or BERTScore.
The static attention based approach outperforms all the baselines on automatic and human evaluation.
The proposed BERT-based evaluation metric DEB significantly outperforms existing models, showing better correlation with human judgments and better performance on random negatives.
Collecting human judgments allows for more nuanced comparisons of AMR generation systems compared to automated metrics.
LEPOR will yield state-of-the-art correlation with human judgments compared to classic metrics BLEU, TER, Meteor-1.3, AMBER, and MP4IBM1.
The correlation between the ranks computed by the SVM based ranking function and human ranking is significantly better than the correlation between ranking of individual features and human ranking.
A similarity metric outperforms alternatives on all tasks considered.
QRelScore will achieve a higher correlation with human judgments while being more robust to adversarial samples compared to existing metrics for question generation.
The ranking method correlates better with real human preferences than baseline models.
UniEval will achieve a higher correlation with human judgments than existing metrics for multi-dimensional evaluation in Natural Language Generation.
The use of multiple references results in improved correlation between several automatic metrics and human judgement for both the quality and the diversity of system output.
Language Model Augmented Relevance Score (MARS) achieves higher correlation with human reference judgements compared to existing metrics.
Automated metrics have stronger correlation with human judgments in the task-oriented setting compared to what has been observed in the non task-oriented setting.
SUM-QE achieves very high correlations with human ratings, outperforming simpler models addressing these linguistic aspects.
The global algorithm achieves substantial improvements over previous work on both pairwise and rank correlation metrics.
ViLBERTScore will correlate significantly better with human judgments than existing metrics for evaluating image captioning systems.
FactPEGASUS substantially improves factuality evaluated by multiple automatic metrics and humans compared to the original pre-training objective.
Human judgement can provide valuable insights for evaluating automatic metrics in formality transfer.
The PARENT metric correlates with human judgments better than existing text generation metrics.
Learning an automatic scoring metric based on human judgments will improve the evaluation of summaries in the summarization field.
Combining multiple models based on heterogeneous information sources will significantly improve the measurement of relational similarity between word pairs.
Batch-mean centering strategy and tempered Word Mover Distance will improve the encoding representations for similarity metrics, achieving state of the art correlation with human ratings.

Models that used human similarity judgments to learn category-specific weights on dimensions yielded substantially better predictions than all unweighted approaches across all types of similarity functions and representations.
The automatic evaluation metric Q^2 will show higher correlation with human judgments compared to other metrics for evaluating factual consistency in knowledge-grounded dialogue.
Using word embeddings to compute semantic similarity will achieve better correlations with human judgements compared to traditional ROUGE evaluation.
The DPP system with improved similarity measure performs competitively, outperforming strong summarization baselines on benchmark datasets.
METEOR will show improved correlation with human judgments of translation quality compared to existing metrics.
GRADE will significantly outperform other state-of-the-art metrics on measuring diverse dialogue models in terms of the Pearson and Spearman correlations with human judgements.
QAFactEval leads to a 14% average improvement over previous QA-based metrics on the SummaC factual consistency benchmark.
The two-stage attribute extractor will surpass several retrieval and generation baselines on human evaluation.
Sentence mover's similarity will correlate with human judgments significantly better than ROUGE for evaluating machine-generated texts.
An embedding-based metric that has the highest correlation with human evaluations on a standard benchmark can have the lowest correlation if the amount of input noise or unknown tokens increases.
The new evaluation measure will significantly outperform state-of-the-art approaches in sentence-level correlation.
EIGEN outperforms strong baselines both in terms of automated evaluation metrics and human judgments on closeness to reference and relevance of generations.
MoverScore shows a high correlation with human judgment of text quality across various text generation tasks.
Taking human judgement as a gold standard, we investigate which metrics have the highest accuracy in predicting translation quality rankings for such system pairs.
The proposed paradigm for evaluating image descriptions using human consensus captures human judgment of consensus better than existing metrics.
An information-content-based measure proposed by Jiang and Conrath is found superior to those proposed by Hirst and St-Onge, Leacock and Chodorow, Lin, and Resnik.
The proposed model for generating informative market comments outperforms the existing model both in BLEU scores and human judgment.
A novel metric to evaluate the factual consistency in text summarization via counterfactual estimation will improve the correlation with human judgments and the convenience of usage.
BEER metric will maximize correlation with human system ranking through efficient tuning of features and novel features that provide smoother sentence level scores.
Meteor has the strongest correlation with human judgements for image description evaluation.

given_idea: Memory-Augmented Recurrent Transformer (MART) generates more coherent and less repetitive paragraph captions than baseline methods, while maintaining relevance to the input video events.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Hierarchical Recurrent Neural Networks will significantly outperform current state-of-the-art methods in video captioning as demonstrated by BLEU@4 scores.
An end-to-end transformer model for dense video captioning will improve the consistency between event proposals and captioning during training.
The multimodal Recurrent Neural Network (m-RNN) model outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generating image captions and retrieval tasks.
Topical Hierarchical Recurrent Encoder Decoder (THRED) will generate more diverse and contextually relevant responses compared to strong baselines.
Hierarchical Recurrent Neural Encoder (HRNE) efficiently exploits video temporal structure in a longer range and outperforms state-of-the-art methods on video captioning benchmarks.
Memory-enhanced RNN decoder will improve translation performance in neural machine translation compared to existing methods.

Long Short-Term Memory with Attributes (LSTM-A) will improve image captioning performance by integrating attributes and inter-attribute correlations into the Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks framework.
Multimodal Transformer Networks will improve the performance of video-grounded dialogue systems compared to existing RNN and sequence-to-sequence architectures.
The key-value memory-augmented attention model for Neural Machine Translation will improve the adequacy of translations compared to existing attention-based models.
The proposed models produce captions that are more diverse and more accurate than a strong LSTM baseline or a 'vanilla' conditional variational auto-encoder with a fixed Gaussian prior.
Synchronized Deep Recurrent Attentive Writer (Sync-DRAW) can generate videos from simple captions and is efficient in learning the spatial and temporal information of the videos.
The coherence recurrent convolutional network (CRCN) outperforms other state-of-the-art candidate methods in retrieving a sequence of natural sentences for an image stream.
The multiscale sequence modeling with a learned dictionary performs better than a regular Long Short-Term Memory on language modeling tasks, especially for smaller models.

A novel sequence modeling approach that exploits explicit and implicit mentions of temporal common sense will produce better representations of events for relevant tasks compared to the standard BERT.
Bidirectional LSTM models achieve highly competitive performance to the state-of-the-art results on caption generation and significantly outperform recent methods on retrieval task.
The LSTM–GAN system architecture will significantly outperform existing methods on standard public datasets for video captioning.
Incorporating high-level concepts into the CNN-RNN approach achieves a significant improvement on the state-of-the-art in both image captioning and visual question answering.
The proposed RNN architecture for sequence labelling will achieve better performance than the state-of-the-art methods.
Convolutional image captioning technique will demonstrate performance on par with the long-short-term-memory baseline while having a faster training time per number of parameters.
Parallel Multi-Scale Attention for Sequence to Sequence Learning (MUSE) achieves substantial performance improvements over Transformer, especially on long sequences.
A stacked residual LSTM network will outperform sequence to sequence, attention-based, and bi-directional LSTM models on paraphrase generation tasks.
Deep Embedded Memory Networks will outperform other question-answering models in video story question-answering tasks.
The re-read LSTM unit will achieve better results than current state-of-the-art methods for recognizing textual entailment.
A novel end-to-end sequence-to-sequence model will generate captions for videos by associating a sequence of video frames to a sequence of words.
Incorporating topic information into the convolutional sequence-to-sequence model will improve coherence, diversity, and informativeness of generated summaries.

The deep neural model integrates feature extraction from convolutional neural networks into sequence modeling in recurrent neural networks, optimized by utterance and dialog level attentions, and outperforms the previous state-of-the-art model on this task in a different genre using bidirectional long short-term memory, showing a 13.0+% improvement for longer dialogs.
The conditional recurrent neural network generates a summary of an input sentence and significantly outperforms the recently proposed state-of-the-art method on the Giga-word corpus.
Using BERT to encode complex semantics from video clips will significantly improve performance in video question answering compared to previous methods.
Providing a contextual real-valued input vector in association with each word will improve the performance of recurrent neural network language models.
The method shows significant improvement over a strong RNN-based encoder-decoder baseline for reducing redundant repeating generation in neural abstractive summarization.
Incorporating high-level concepts and external knowledge into the CNN-RNN approach will significantly improve performance in image captioning and visual question answering.
Adapting Transformer to end-to-end spoken language translation will outperform a strong recurrent neural network-based baseline with a significant reduction in training time.
Applying sequence-level training with an integrated penalty on trigram repetition will produce more diverse paragraphs in image paragraph captioning.
M² - a Meshed Transformer with Memory for Image Captioning achieves a new state of the art in single-model and ensemble configurations on the 'Karpathy' test split and on the online test server.
The pointer sentinel-LSTM model achieves state of the art language modeling performance on the Penn Treebank while using far fewer parameters than a standard softmax LSTM.
A pretrained sequence-to-sequence model for document ranking will outperform encoder-only pretrained transformer architectures such as BERT in terms of relevance probabilities for ranking.
An attention mechanism allows a Recurrent Neural Network to learn alignments between sequences of input frames and output labels for Large Vocabulary Continuous Speech Recognition.
The Context Sequence Memory Network (CSMN) will enhance personalized image captioning performance compared to state-of-the-art captioning models.
The RNMT+ model outperforms the classic RNN-based approaches, convolutional seq2seq model, and Transformer model on benchmark tasks.
The transformer and sequence-to-sequence model complement themselves adequately, making for a richer encoded vector representation in single document summarization.
Transformer achieves significant performance benefits compared to recurrent neural networks in automatic speech recognition benchmarks.
A copy-augmented sequence-to-sequence architecture will outperform more complex memory-augmented models in task-oriented dialogue performance.
Gated recurrent units are better than traditional recurrent units for sequence modeling tasks.
Recurrently stacked sequence-to-sequence models will achieve translation quality comparable to models with multiple distinct layers while using significantly fewer parameters.
Local recurrent neural network enhanced transformer will outperform baseline transformer in terms of mean opinion score and case level intelligibility.
An extended model of active memory will match existing attention models on neural machine translation and generalize better to longer sentences.

The STMC-Transformer improves translation performance over existing methods by over 5 and 7 BLEU on gloss-to-text and video-to-text translation respectively, and outperforms gloss supervision methods.
LSTM-based encoders perform better than dilated CNNs, Transformers, and BERT in contract element extraction.
Generating captions based on part-of-speech summaries will achieve high accuracy, faster computation, and high diversity compared to traditional beam search methods.
Modeling recurrence for Transformer with an additional recurrence encoder will improve the translation capacity compared to the standard Transformer model.
